channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Elon Musk announces Terafab project he claims will be the 'largest chip manufacturing facility ever'
item.description: Elon Musk has announced the Terafab project, a joint venture between Tesla, SpaceX and xAI, to build the "largest chip manufacturing facility ever." In his usual grandiose fashion, Musk claims Terafab is the next step towards harnessing the power of the sun and creating a "galactic civilization." Musk, CEO of all three companies, announced plans for the Terafab in a livestream on X. As the name implies, the project's ultimate goal is to produce a terawatt of computing power each year so that it can match the companies' growing demand for chips. Musk explained during the livestream that he's gr...

item.title: Even the most advanced AI models fail more often than you think on structured outputs — raising doubts about the effectiveness of coding assistants
item.description: Large language models struggle with structured outputs, achieving only 75% accuracy on complex tasks, leaving reliability concerns for developers.

item.title: These are the 5 hidden macOS settings I can't live without when playing games on my Mac
item.description: Improve your Mac’s gaming performance with these helpful tips and changes.

item.title: Ubisoft took trips to Japan with expert guides for Assassin's Creed Shadows and spent 'a lot of time' researching on top of that — 'We're making video games, but for Assassin's Creed we try to be credible'
item.description: Ubisoft has revealed that it did extensive research for the development of Assassin's Creed Shadows and even took guided trips around Japan for inspiration.

item.title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra vs iPhone 17 Pro Max : comment choisir le meilleur smartphone premium ?
item.description: Xiaomi se frotte une nouvelle fois aux ténors du marché avec son modèle 17 Ultra qui, c'est la magie des nombres, s'oppose frontalement à l'iPhone 17 Pro Max. Lequel est le meilleur ? On les compare pour vous aider à choisir !

item.title: The best early deals you can already grab from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day sale may arrive earlier than usual this year, but there’s a deal event happening much sooner: Amazon’s annual Big Spring Sale, which is happening March 25th through the 31st. While it won’t be as big as Prime Day or Black Friday, it does offer you a chance to save money at a […]

item.title: Ces écouteurs JBL open-ear pensés pour le sport en promo à -38 % donnent tout sur l’autonomie et le confort
item.description: Les JBL Sense Lite sont des écouteurs sans-fil à conduction aérienne, d'une grande légèreté, que vous retrouvez à 61,50 euros sur Amazon au lieu de 99,99 euros.

item.title: Open Channel: Tell Us What You Thought of ‘Project Hail Mary’
item.description: Now that 'Project Hail Mary' has blasted off into theaters, was it as good or you hoped, or a complete disaster?

item.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
item.description: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber everywhere, all at once
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

item.title: “We wanted to focus on those three things… not always done by our competitors when they put out products” – I talked to Intel about its plans for 2026, Panther Lake, and beyond
item.description: Intel’s Nish Neelalojanan on Panther Lake performance and expectations.

item.title: Testing autonomous agents (Or: how I learned to stop worrying and embrace chaos)
item.description: Look, we've spent the last 18 months building production AI systems, and we'll tell you what keeps us up at night — and it's not whether the model can answer questions. That's table stakes now. What haunts us is the mental image of an agent autonomously approving a six-figure vendor contract at 2 a.m. because someone typo'd a config file.We've moved past the era of "ChatGPT wrappers" (thank God), but the industry still treats autonomous agents like they're just chatbots with API access. They're not. When you give an AI system the ability to take actions without human confirmation, you're cross...
